highlights.text: I wrote last Wednesday at 4pm: “My general lean is ES can try some back-testing.
I wrote on Monday at 4pm: “My general lean is to defer to the trend.
Bulls want to hold 6943-45 in a strong bull case (or quick trap down to 6925).
My general lean is bulls want to hold 6973, 6948 lowest.
ES behaves according to what I call the two siblings: Elevator down and short squeeze.
Elevator down refers to a period where ES sells rapidly, ignoring supports in the process.
Every point of elevator down sell is nearly paid back to bulls in the form of an equally or more violent short squeeze.
However, the rallies do not occur randomly, they only occur when ES puts in my core setup: The Failed Breakdown, and Failed Breakdowns represent institutional accumulation.
Yesterday, I was looking for more on this, but we would again need another Failed Breakdown.

If 6948 fails, we sell.” We saw just this today - we dipped mildly overnight/this morning, then ran to 7000-02 by noon before topping out and going elevator down into the close and seeing a dip finally.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (which produced the squeeze to 7k+) and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: After 3 Green Days, SPX Sees A Late Day Dip. Will Bulls Buy It? January 8 Plan
